MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SM CONSTRUCTION & ASSOCIATES, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Hearing continued to March 19, 2010 in Department 301. Judge Woolard recuses herself. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defts To Provide Responses To Written Discovery And For An Order Establishing Admissions And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Keith M. Velleca, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: SM CONSTRUCTION & ASSOCIATES, Inc.

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF SM CONSTRUCTION & ASSOCIATES, INC's Motion To Compel Defendants BACE Development, LLC And Amy Liu To Provide Responses To Written Discovery And For An Order Establishing Admissions is granted as Defendants are ordered to respond to Form and Special Interrogatories and Requests For Production Of Documents within 15 days of notice of this order. The motion is denied as it seeks an order establishing the truth of the Requests For Admission as responses have been made.
The failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendants, jointly and severally, in the amount of $790, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant's Counsel Martin M. Eisenberg for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar and dissolved/fee paid. Proceedings not reported. (212)

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK N.A. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Civil Code section 3264 bars this claim. Plaintiffs have not alleged the existence of a direct written contract. No reasonable probability that the defect can be cured by amendment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
